commit.message:
Fix CWT: wire Supabase secrets to vitest runner + document MAT_E2E_TEST_TOKEN setup (#976)

* Initial plan

* chore(iaa): pre-brief wave-cwt-envvars — CI_WORKFLOW + AAWP_MAT trigger classification, FFA checks, PREHANDOVER structure, 4 scope blockers declared

Pre-Brief artifact for wave wave-cwt-envvars (branch: copilot/fix-supabase-env-vars-for-tests).

Tasks in scope:
- T-CWT-EV-001: Add env block to CWT Run Combined Wave Tests step (CI_WORKFLOW trigger)
- T-CWT-EV-002: Document CWT env var setup in BUILD_PROGRESS_TRACKER.md (AAWP_MAT trigger)

PR category: MIXED (CI_WORKFLOW + AAWP_MAT)
Blocking FFA checks declared: OVL-CI-001–005, BD-001, BD-002, BD-003, BD-016
4 preventable blockers declared (CI evidence, no hardcoded secrets, SCOPE_DECLARATION, A-021)
IAA token file path pre-declared: .agent-admin/assurance/iaa-token-session-cwt-envvars-wave-cwt-envvars-20260307.md

Phase 0 PRE-BRIEF mode — Phases 1–4 not executed.
Authority: CS2 (@APGI-cmy)

* feat(cwt): add step-level env vars for Supabase secrets in CWT test step

T-CWT-EV-001: Add env: block (step-level only) to 'Run Combined Wave Tests'
step in deploy-mat-ai-gateway.yml. Wires VITE_SUPABASE_URL,
VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, and MAT_E2E_TEST_TOKEN from GitHub Actions secrets.
Scoped to the test step only — not the cwt: job level.

T-CWT-EV-002: Append Wave CWT-EnvVars documentation section to
BUILD_PROGRESS_TRACKER.md covering: how to generate Supabase JWT,
where to add GitHub secrets, env-var wiring details, and expected
test outcomes (8 tests expected GREEN; T-W13-E2E-1 out of scope).

No hardcoded secrets. BD-016 compliant. SCOPE_DECLARATION.md updated.

Branch: copilot/fix-supabase-env-vars-for-tests

* chore(foreman): PREHANDOVER proof + session memory for wave-cwt-envvars

Finding: SCOPE_DECLARATION.md fails validate-scope-to-diff.sh (BL-027 / A-026).

  file entries to fail parser (A-028 format non-compliance)
- Root Cause B: 3 ceremony files (PREHANDOVER, session memory, parking-station) added
  in commit cfd3963 not reflected in SCOPE_DECLARATION.md

All substantive checks PASS. Workflow fix correct and safe. Documentation BD-016 compliant.
Pure ceremony admin fix required.

Token file: .agent-admin/assurance/iaa-token-session-cwt-envvars-wave-cwt-envvars-20260307.md

commit.message:
feat(hooks): add hook system (PRE/POST_TOOL, SESSION, PRE_COMPACT) + RBAC + API

- platform/hooks/__init__.py: HookRegistry singleton, HookType enum, HookContext/HookResult dataclasses, fire_pre() (can block) + fire() (non-blocking)
- platform/hooks/builtins.py: 6 built-in hooks — pre_compact, session_start, session_end, pattern_extract, quality_gate, cost_tracker
- platform/hooks/rbac.py: can_register_hook() — blocking PRE_TOOL requires scope=platform or security/arch category
- platform/agents/executor.py: wired SESSION_START, PRE_TOOL (blocking), POST_TOOL, PRE_COMPACT, SESSION_END at all 2 tool-call sites
- platform/web/routes/api/hooks.py: GET/POST/DELETE/PATCH /api/hooks + GET /api/hooks/log + /api/hooks/types
- platform/web/routes/api/__init__.py: include hooks_router
- platform/db/migrations.py: hook_registrations + hook_log tables (SQLite + PostgreSQL)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Script to verify all deployment configuration is correct.
Checks: vercel.json, api/, client/dist/, package.json files,
build configuration, and local functionality.

All checks should pass for successful Vercel deployment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(executor): allow Claude alias read tools in write-only nudge mode

read_file/write_file/read_many_files/edit_file added to write-only tool list
so MiniMax can read context before writing. Prevents REJECTED rejections
that caused agents to give up after 0-1 code_write calls.

OVH fix (streaming path): removed write-only stripping entirely,
extended force condition to (_force_tc_attempts < 2 or write_count < 3).

commit.message:
feat: agent drag-to-reorder, order badge, remove composer dropdown (UI-010) (#42)

* feat(UI-010): agent drag-to-reorder, order badge, remove composer dropdown

- Add sort_order column to agent table in schema.sql (DEFAULT 0)
- Add idempotent ALTER TABLE migration in db.py for existing databases
- Add PATCH /agents/{id}/order endpoint (validates non-negative, returns 400/404)
- Update GET /agents to order by sort_order ASC, display_name ASC
- Add sort_order to _AGENT_KEYS and Agent/AgentData types
- Add reorderAgent() to api/client.ts
- Remove agent-selector dropdown and targetAgentId from Composer.tsx
- Simplify ComposerProps.onSend to (message: string) => void
- Remove Target First <select> from ChatPane.tsx
- Add circular order badge (1-indexed) on AgentCard with brand gold CSS
- Implement HTML5 native drag-to-reorder in AgentRoster with drag handle
- AgentRoster manages local order state with render-phase sync from props
- Update AppShell.tsx toAgentData mapper and onSend wiring
- Fix pre-existing personalities.json prettier formatting
- Update test_schema.py and test_db_manager.py for new column
- Add TestReorderAgent backend test class (6 tests)
- Add AgentCard order badge tests, AgentRoster sort/drag tests
- Update Composer tests to expect no dropdown

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(UI-010): guard fromIdx/toIdx against -1 in handleDrop

Prevents splice(-1, 1) from silently moving the last element when a
stale drag ref or race condition causes findIndex to return -1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(UI-011): playbook, saga, indexes, and tracking files

Renamed from UI-010 to UI-011 to avoid collision with API connectivity fix (UI-010).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(UI-011): remove duplicate _apply_migrations, fix /api prefix in reorder tests

Cherry-pick from UI-010 branch introduced a duplicate _apply_migrations()
overload after merging with PR #41 (which already had the versioned variant).
Removed the stale no-arg overload; call site now consistently uses the
from_version signature.

Also fixed TestReorderAgent test paths: /agents/ -> /api/agents/ to match
the /api prefix added by PR #41.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use GraphQL API to assign Copilot bot to issues

The REST API --assignee 'copilot' fails because Copilot is a bot
account. Switch to actions/github-script with GraphQL
addAssigneesToAssignable mutation, with fallback to @copilot comment.

Requires COPILOT_PAT secret (PAT with repo + copilot permissions).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove \n from Mermaid node labels — use short single-line labels

PA flow diagram had \n in 6 node labels. Replaced with concise
single-line text per the Mermaid guidelines.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(api): extract pricing tier utilities — deduplicate pricing logic (E-03)

Extract shared pricing_tiers parsing, price extraction, and paddle_price_id
lookup into pricing-utils.ts. Replaces duplicated logic across 4 files:

- dashboard/route.ts: JSON string parsing + first tier price
- billing/overview/route.ts: price-id matching + first tier fallback
- subscription/update/route.ts: package lookup by price-id
- subscription-updated.ts: same package lookup by price-id

5 utility functions: getDisplayPrice, parsePricingTiers, getFirstTier,
findTierByPriceId, findPackageIdByPriceId.

Net: +68 lines (utility) / -86 lines (routes) = -18 lines total.

fix: Remove vercel.json and let Vercel auto-detect configuration

The persistent DEPLOYMENT_NOT_FOUND error suggests Vercel's custom
configuration may be causing issues. Remove vercel.json entirely and
let Vercel use its auto-detection for:
- /api/* routes (auto-detect from api/ directory)
- Frontend static files (auto-detect from public/ or build output)

Vercel should auto-detect:
- API routes from api/index.js
- Build command from root package.json
- Frontend output from client/dist

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Move server.js to api/index.js for proper Vercel serverless routing

Vercel expects API routes to be in /api directory for serverless functions.
Moved server.js to api/index.js and updated vercel.json to use the standard
Vercel routing structure.

This should properly trigger Vercel's automatic API route detection.

Remove superfluous pr:: sub-namespace qualifications

Targeted removal of pr::(sub-namespace):: patterns across 65 C++ files
where the pr:: prefix is redundant (code is already inside namespace pr).

Only sub-namespace-qualified references were changed (e.g. pr::gui::ListView
to gui::ListView). Bare type references like pr::v4 were left untouched
to avoid shadowing issues.

Safety rules applied:
- Only inside namespace pr blocks (brace-depth tracked)
- Skipped #include, namespace, and #define lines (including multi-line macros)
- Skipped unittests blocks (test namespaces shadow parent names)
- Preserved UTF-8 BOM encoding

commit.message:
Merge pull request #6 from dchu3/fix/my-wallet-address

fix: inject wallet public address into system prompt

Derive the user's wallet public address from SOLANA_PRIVATE_KEY at
startup and include it in the Gemini system instruction. This allows
the LLM to resolve "my wallet" / "my balance" to the correct on-chain
address instead of guessing or returning 0.

Changes:
- Add bs58 dependency for Base58 encoding/decoding
- config.ts: derive walletAddress from the last 32 bytes of the keypair
- agent.ts: accept walletAddress param, interpolate into system prompt
- index.ts: pass config.walletAddress through to runAgent()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Signed-off-by: Arun Kumar Kushwaha <austen.dezigns.dev@gmail.com>

fix: restore template-managed files missing from target during sync (#375)

* Initial plan

* fix: restore template-managed files missing from target after sync

When the template snapshot is unchanged since the last sync (no diff to
apply), `_merge_with_base` returns early without copying any files.
If template-managed files were absent from the target repository they
were silently filtered out of the lock instead of being restored.

After the merge/copy step in `_sync_merge`, check for any materialized
files that are absent from the target and copy them from the upstream
snapshot. This covers both the "template unchanged" and the
"changes applied" paths.

Fixes the warning:
  "N file(s) in lock absent from target and excluded: ..."

Co-authored-by: tschm <2046079+tschm@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: tschm <2046079+tschm@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: 2026 renderer overhaul — 29 core modules + 10 game upgrades

Complete next-generation WebGPU rendering stack with:

Core Engine (29 modules, ~19K lines):
- Monte Carlo path tracer with BVH, MIS, NEE, Russian Roulette
- Neural denoiser (firefly suppress, à-trous wavelet, temporal accumulation)
- Radiance cascades GI with 5-cascade SH-encoded probe grids
- Volumetric path tracer with 128³ 3D noise (Perlin/Worley/curl)
- PBR 2.0 material system with 27 horror presets, SSS, iridescence
- Nanite-style virtual geometry (10M+ triangles via cluster LOD)
- GPU-driven pipeline with zero-CPU-overhead indirect draws
- Render graph with topological sort, dead-pass elimination
- Area lights (7 types, clustered 16×9×24, LTC, 8 IES profiles)
- Atmospheric renderer (Rayleigh+Mie, volumetric clouds, weather)
- HDR pipeline (histogram exposure, ACES, bloom, Display-P3)
- TSR temporal upscaler with Halton jitter and RCAS sharpening
- Variable rate shading (foveated + content/motion adaptive)
- Tessellation with POM self-shadowing and 5 horror animations
- Virtual texturing with LRU page cache and mipmap streaming
- Emissive light propagation volumes with SH encoding
- Async compute pipeline with topological scheduling
- Memory manager with ring buffer sub-allocation and defrag
- Mobile WebGPU with 4 device tiers and thermal throttling
- Psychological horror shaders (8 effects: uncanny valley, etc.)
- Fourth wall breaker (Voronoi cracks, data moshing, persistence)
- Audio-reactive visuals (heartbeat vignette, bass shake)
- Haptic visual feedback (screen shake, damage indicators)
- Biometric-reactive rendering with safety limits
- Horror replay system with delta compression
- Cross-game corruption with 9 game profiles
- Dynamic destruction (Voronoi fracture, structural cascades)
- Procedural horror art (6 style presets, per-player seeding)

Game Upgrades (10 files, ~10K lines):
- Shadow Crawler 3D: path-traced lighting, volumetric fog, 20 levels
- Backrooms Pac-Man: fluorescent flicker, moist carpet PBR, reality glitch
- Blood Tetris: 3D tetrominoes, blood splatter compute, heartbeat veins
- Hellaphobia: reality warping, SSS creatures, 21-phase volumetric fog
- Caribbean Conquest: FFT ocean, ship destruction, dynamic weather
- The Deep: Beer-Lambert absorption, bioluminescence, pressure effects
- The Elevator: 25 floor presets, mirror reflections, sanity degradation
- Zombie Horde: 12K GPU-instanced zombies, ragdoll dismemberment
- Haunted Asylum: ghost rendering, wet floor reflections, destruction
- Remaining 17 games: universal upgrade with per-game presets

commit.message:
fix(data): add missing ThrowIfDisposed guards in 4 repositories + COTReportService null check

BUG FIX 2026-07: Architecture audit found 36 public methods across 4 repositories
(AssetPerformanceRepository, HistoricalDataRepository, PaperTradingRepository,
PerformanceReportRepository) missing ThrowIfDisposed() guards before acquiring
DbContextWriteLock. Without these guards, a disposed repository could access a
disposed DbContext, causing ObjectDisposedException or data corruption.

Also added ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull guard to COTReportService.SetConfig()
to prevent null config from causing NullReferenceException on IsReady property access.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Simplify vercel.json to use single build command

The @vercel/static-build builder was causing deployment issues with Vite.

New approach:
1. Single buildCommand: 'npm run build' (uses root package.json)
2. Single @vercel/node builder for server/server.js
3. Root package.json script handles both client and server builds
4. Routes point directly to client/dist for static files
5. API routes still go to server/server.js

This is simpler and more reliable than the dual-builder approach.

commit.message:
fix: correct model_id on failure + add feature timeout (F05, F13)

F05: Model runner was hardcoding model_id to 'claude' for ALL failed
evaluations regardless of which model failed. Now uses the index from
Promise.allSettled to map to the correct model ID. Fixes corrupted
drift detection and per-model accuracy tracking.

F13: Feature computation Promise.all had no timeout — if Yahoo hangs,
the entire evaluation hangs indefinitely. Now wrapped with withTimeout
at 15s using the existing retry.ts utility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Settings page completeness audit: add Reset buttons and missing trading fields

- Add Reset buttons to 8 sections missing them: PPO, Quantum ML, AutoML,
  TimeGAN, VAE, FinGPT, Agent, Risk management
- Implement 8 new Reset methods using CreateDefault() factory methods
- Add asset-specific ATR multiplier settings (Gold, Crypto, Forex SL/TP)
- Add extended cooldown settings (duration + consecutive loss threshold)
- Wire new fields through load (OnInitializedAsync) and save (SaveTradingParamsAsync)
- All sections now have working Save + Reset buttons

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Include client/dist in git and fix gitignore

The root .gitignore was ignoring all 'dist' directories, which prevented
client/dist from being tracked in git. Vercel was building from source,
but the build might have been failing.

Solution:
1. Change gitignore to only ignore server/dist (not client/dist)
2. Force-add client/dist to git repo
3. This ensures Vercel has the built client artifacts

Changes:
- Updated .gitignore to: /server/dist instead of dist
- Added client/dist/ to git repo

commit.message:
[phase-5] polish pexels-media: document local env source

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ml): LSTM batch training parity, PPO advantage normalization, VAE dispose safety

- LSTM TrainAssetWithReducedEpochsAsync: Switch from full-batch to mini-batch
  training to prevent CUDA OOM on larger datasets (matches main TrainAsync)
- LSTM TrainAssetWithReducedEpochsAsync: Add NaN/Infinity validation on loss
  values to prevent silent model corruption
- LSTM TrainAssetWithReducedEpochsAsync: Add early stopping support
- LSTM TrainAssetWithReducedEpochsAsync: Dispose old model before creating new
  one when continueTraining=false (prevents GPU memory leak)
- PPO: Add advantage normalization (zero-mean, unit-variance) - standard PPO
  best practice for stable policy gradient updates
- VAE: Fix Dispose race - set _isReady=false and null _model before disposing
  to prevent TOCTOU where AnalyzeAsync reads disposed model
- ONNX: Add empty batch guard in PredictBatch to prevent zero-length tensor crash
- Fix pre-existing build errors from other agents (FeatureSelectionService
  IReadOnlyList param, OnlineLearningService readonly field, TradingStrategy
  IReadOnlyList.GetRange)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
